name: Lleu Llaw Gyffes
description: Lleu Llaw Gyffes, meaning 'Bright One with a Skillful Hand,' is a prominent hero in Welsh mythology, primarily known from the Fourth Branch of the Mabinogi. Born to Arianrhod, he was disowned by her at birth and denied a name, arms, and a wife. Through the cunning of his uncle Gwydion, Lleu received all three, circumventing Arianrhod's curses. Gwydion tricked Arianrhod into naming him Lleu, and later, by illusion, obtained arms and armor for him. To fulfill the curse of not having a mortal wife, Gwydion and Math fab Mathonwy created Blodeuwedd, a beautiful woman made of flowers. However, Blodeuwedd betrayed Lleu by plotting his death with her lover, Gronw Pebyr. Lleu could only be killed under very specific and peculiar circumstances, which Blodeuwedd tricked him into revealing. Despite being mortally wounded by Gronw, Lleu did not die. Instead, he transformed into an eagle and flew away. Gwydion eventually found him, restored him to human form, and avenged his betrayal by killing Gronw Pebyr and turning Blodeuwedd into an owl. Lleu then reclaimed his lands and ruled wisely, becoming a symbol of resilience, skill, and the triumph of good over treachery in Welsh lore. He represents the power of perseverance and the importance of cunning in overcoming seemingly insurmountable obstacles.
subtitle: The Bright One with a Skillful Hand
achievements: Received a name despite Arianrhod's curse
Obtained arms and armor through Gwydion's trickery
Survived Blodeuwedd's betrayal and Gronw's attack
Returned to rule his lands after transformation

keyMyths.title: The Naming of Lleu
keyMyths.description: Arianrhod refuses to name her son, but Gwydion tricks her by disguising himself and Lleu as shoemakers and winning her over with his craft. She unwittingly names him 'Lleu' while admiring his skill.

keyMyths.title: The Creation of Blodeuwedd
keyMyths.description: To circumvent Arianrhod's curse that Lleu would never have a human wife, Gwydion and Math create a beautiful woman named Blodeuwedd from the flowers of oak, broom, and meadowsweet.

keyMyths.title: The Betrayal and Transformation
keyMyths.description: Blodeuwedd plots with Gronw Pebyr to kill Lleu, tricking him into revealing the specific circumstances under which he can be slain. He is wounded and transforms into an eagle.

sources.author: Gwyn Jones and Thomas Jones
sources.description: A translation of the medieval Welsh tales, including the Fourth Branch, which features Lleu Llaw Gyffes prominently.
sources.text: A translation of the medieval Welsh tales, including the Fourth Branch, which features Lleu Llaw Gyffes prominently.

sources.author: John Matthews
sources.work: Taliesin: Shamanism and the Bardic Mysteries in Britain and Ireland
sources.description: Explores the connections between Lleu and the figure of Taliesin, linking him to bardic traditions.
